claims.guidance: When child click handlers do not fire, validate input flags across the parent/template chain, not only on the child target.
claims.statement: Enabling handleinput on a child may not be sufficient if an ancestor container or template instance is not input-enabled.

claims.guidance: For collapsed first-render scroll layouts, size from the outer parent first, then resize children and update the scroll rect.
claims.statement: Early show/layout reads on nested content or scroll-child windows can be smaller than eventual usable space.

confidence.rationale: Observed and user-validated during addon implementation, but still framed as behavior guidance rather than canonical engine guarantee.

evidence.summary: Child click target only worked after parent row template input handling was enabled.
Details scroll region initially showed one row until layout derived height from outer window and refreshed scroll rect.

criteria: Corroborate in additional addons or canonical references.
Retain MEDIUM confidence unless broader evidence is collected.
promotion.notes: Promote as XML behavior caveats, not new API symbols.

source.context: Healer details window implementation and in-game user validation.

claims.guidance: Treat the table attribute as the backing collection for list rows, and rebuild that Lua table before refreshing or reordering the list.
claims.statement: ListData appears to bind a ListBox to a named Lua table through its table attribute.

claims.guidance: Use windowname and variable to bind text-bearing row children from structured table entries, and keep format declarations conservative.
claims.statement: ListColumn entries under ListData appear to map fields from each backing-table entry onto row-template child windows.

claims.guidance: Use ListColumns for straightforward text binding, and reserve populationfunction for extra row work such as icons or row-specific state that is not expressed directly in XML.
claims.statement: When populationfunction is present, Lua can perform custom row population and use PopulatorIndices plus ListBox APIs to manage visible-row ordering and row-to-data mapping.

claims.guidance: If the list only needs ListColumns text binding, an empty populationfunction may be enough; add a Lua population function only when the row requires extra runtime setup.
claims.statement: A blank populationfunction appears to be accepted when XML column binding alone is sufficient for the list.

confidence.rationale: Observed repeatedly in addon implementations, but ListData itself is not yet covered by canonical platform documentation.

evidence.summary: QuickTacticSwitch ListBox declares ListData with a Lua table path and population function.
QuickTacticSwitch ListColumns binds Name and Enemy fields from the Lua row table to row-template child windows.
QuickTacticSwitch builds QTS.listDisplayData before refreshing the list.
QuickTacticSwitch sets visible list ordering with ListBoxSetDisplayOrder.
QuickTacticSwitch PopulateDisplay iterates QuickTacticSwitchWindowList.PopulatorIndices and sets row icons.
QuickTacticSwitch uses ListBoxGetDataIndex to map a clicked row back to the current data entry.
AggroMeter declares a ListData node with an empty populationfunction value for a text-only list.

criteria: Corroborate in additional addons or canonical references before promoting callback semantics beyond the observed binding model.
Keep MEDIUM confidence unless canonical platform documentation or broader evidence is added.
promotion.notes: Promote as an observed XML list-binding pattern, not as a guaranteed engine-side symbol contract.

source.context: XML and Lua implementation review of QuickTacticSwitch, corroborated against other in-workspace addons using ListBox/ListData patterns.

claims.guidance: If a control renders in the wrong column or outside the window, clear its anchors and re-anchor it from `$parent` or a stable container such as `$parentButtonBackground` with explicit offsets.
claims.statement: When footer controls or edit boxes drift under sibling-based anchoring, anchoring them directly to a stable parent container is more reliable than chaining them through neighboring controls.

claims.guidance: Use `LabelGetTextDimensions()` and then resize or re-anchor neighboring labels and edit boxes to avoid truncation, overlap, or misplaced values.
claims.statement: Text-driven header or form rows are more stable when label dimensions are measured after LabelSetText and adjacent controls are positioned from the measured width.

claims.guidance: If alternating row tint stops short, increase the row template window and its child label widths instead of only changing the ListBox anchors.
claims.statement: A ListBox row highlight or tint only spans the width of the row template; widening the list box alone does not widen the tinted row background.

claims.guidance: Prefer keeping button height around 39px and increase the containing button-background height before squeezing stacked rows closer together.
claims.statement: Default resizable WAR buttons render more cleanly at their native full height and need adequate footer/container height to prevent stacked controls from overlapping.

confidence.rationale: Observed across multiple UI fixes in Aura and validated in game by the user, but still best treated as platform guidance rather than a hard engine contract.

evidence.summary: Aura profile controls stopped drifting only after edit boxes and value labels were re-anchored directly to stable parent windows and footer containers.
Aura used LabelGetTextDimensions with runtime WindowSetDimensions and re-anchoring to keep `Active Profile:` and `Profile Name:` rows readable during in-game validation.
The profile list row tint did not span the full width until the row template and row label widths were widened, even though the ListBox itself was already wider.
Stacked footer buttons in Aura overlapped or felt cramped until the button background height was increased and full-height button sizing was preserved during in-game review.

criteria: Keep MEDIUM confidence unless corroborated by more addons or canonical platform documentation.
Treat these as practical layout patterns for WAR XML UI work.
promotion.notes: Promote as XML layout guidance and caveats, not as canonical engine guarantees.

source.context: Named profile UI implementation and repeated in-game user validation while fixing layout, spacing, and text placement.

review.notes: Human-validated in game during Aura profile UI implementation; promote as MEDIUM-confidence WAR XML layout guidance.

claims.guidance: Always check Cursor.IconOnCursor() first. Guard with Cursor.Data.Source == Cursor.SOURCE_ACTION_LIST before reading Cursor.Data.ObjectId. Call Cursor.Clear() after every drop handling path (success or rejection) to avoid leaving a stale cursor icon.
claims.statement: When an ability is dragged from the action bar: Cursor.IconOnCursor() returns true; Cursor.Data.Source equals Cursor.SOURCE_ACTION_LIST; Cursor.Data.ObjectId contains the ability ID as an integer. After handling the drop, Cursor.Clear() must be called to consume the cursor item.

confidence.rationale: Cursor.Data.Source, Cursor.Data.ObjectId, and Cursor.SOURCE_ACTION_LIST were used and produced correct ability IDs at runtime. User-validated end-to-end.

evidence.summary: AdvancedPetAssist cast blacklist panel: user dragged abilities from the action bar onto the drop slot. Cursor.IconOnCursor() was true, Cursor.Data.Source equalled Cursor.SOURCE_ACTION_LIST, and Cursor.Data.ObjectId held the correct ability ID. The ability was correctly added to the blacklist. User confirmed the feature works.

criteria: Corroborate Cursor.Data.Source constants for other drag sources (items, gear) in additional addons.
promotion.notes: Cursor.Data subfields (Source, ObjectId) and the SOURCE_ACTION_LIST constant are undocumented in the Cursor table doc. This fills the drag-drop ability identification gap.

source.context: Drag-and-drop ability slot in AdvancedPetAssist cast blacklist UI. User dragged abilities from the action bar onto the drop slot. The correct ability IDs were captured and persisted. User confirmed feature works as intended.

review.notes: Runtime-validated in AdvancedPetAssist. User confirmed UI and behavior work correctly.

claims.guidance: Always save Lua addon files as UTF-8 without BOM. In PowerShell 5.1, do NOT use Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 (it adds a BOM). Use [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(path, content, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)) instead. To verify: read the first 3 bytes — they must NOT be 239 187 191.
claims.statement: The WAR engine Lua parser rejects .lua files that begin with a UTF-8 BOM (byte sequence 0xEF 0xBB 0xBF). The error message is: Error Reading Lua buffer <file>: [string ...]:1: '=' expected near '»'. The '»' character is the visual artefact of the BOM rendered in the error log.

confidence.rationale: BOM-caused parse error observed at runtime with exact error message. Removal of BOM fixed the error. Definitive negative test.

evidence.summary: After truncating APAGui.lua with Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 in PowerShell 5.1, the game logged: Error Reading Lua buffer Interface/AddOns/AdvancedPetAssist/APAGui.lua: [string ...]:1: '=' expected near '»'. Byte inspection confirmed first 3 bytes were the UTF-8 BOM. Re-saving with [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false) resolved the error immediately.

promotion.notes: Promote as a Lua conventions caveat. This is an operational pitfall when editing WAR addon files on Windows using PowerShell tools and affects any addon developer.

source.context: APAGui.lua was re-saved using PowerShell Set-Content -Encoding UTF8 during a file-repair step. The game reported a parse error on next load. Removing the BOM fixed the error immediately.

review.notes: Runtime-validated in AdvancedPetAssist. User confirmed UI and behavior work correctly.

claims.guidance: Use row=1, col=1 for the heading line with COLOR_HEADING applied. Use row=2+ for body lines. The row index is 1-based. Do NOT call Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip — that function does not exist and causes a nil-call runtime error.
claims.statement: The standard pattern for a multi-line text-only tooltip is: Tooltips.CreateTextOnlyTooltip(windowName, nil) then Tooltips.SetTooltipText(row, col, wstring) for each line, Tooltips.SetTooltipColorDef(1, 1, Tooltips.COLOR_HEADING) for the heading row, then Tooltips.Finalize() and Tooltips.AnchorTooltip(Tooltips.ANCHOR_WINDOW_RIGHT). The MouseOverEnd handler should call Tooltips.ClearTooltip().

claims.guidance: Never call Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip. To add a separator or body line, call Tooltips.SetTooltipText with the next row index instead.
claims.statement: Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip does not exist in the WAR engine. Calling it produces the runtime error: attempt to call field 'AddLineToTooltip' (a nil value).

claims.guidance: Pass the table from GetAbilityData(id) as the first argument. Use Tooltips.ANCHOR_WINDOW_RIGHT as anchor. The fifth argument appears unused (nil). Corroborate in additional addons before treating as a hard contract.
claims.statement: Tooltips.CreateAbilityTooltip(abilityData, windowName, anchor, hintWstring, nil) creates an ability tooltip anchored to a named window. The first argument is the table returned by GetAbilityData(id).

confidence.rationale: CreateTextOnlyTooltip + SetTooltipText + Finalize + AnchorTooltip pattern rendered correctly in-game and is user-validated. AddLineToTooltip nil error is a hard runtime fact.

evidence.summary: Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip raised a nil-call error at runtime in AdvancedPetAssist during cast blacklist panel implementation. After removing it, the CreateTextOnlyTooltip + SetTooltipText + Finalize + AnchorTooltip pattern rendered correctly in-game on hover. User confirmed the entire blacklist UI works as intended.

criteria: Corroborate Tooltips.CreateAbilityTooltip signature in additional addons before upgrading to HIGH.
Retain HIGH confidence for the negative AddLineToTooltip fact — runtime error is definitive.
promotion.notes: Promote to war_api_facts.md. The Tooltips namespace is entirely absent from docs/war-api. These are the first documented facts about it.

source.context: Drag-and-drop cast blacklist UI in AdvancedPetAssist. Hover tooltips on the drop slot and ability rows were exercised in-game. User confirmed both UI and behavior work as intended. Tooltips.AddLineToTooltip nil error was observed in a prior iteration, confirming that function does not exist.

review.notes: Runtime-validated in AdvancedPetAssist. User confirmed UI and behavior work correctly.

claims.guidance: Set draganddrop="true" on the Button element in XML. In the OnLButtonUp Lua handler, check Cursor.IconOnCursor() and guard on Cursor.Data.Source before reading the payload. Ensure handleinput="true" or handleinput is not explicitly disabled on the button and its parent chain.
claims.statement: Adding draganddrop="true" to an XML Button element enables it to receive drag-and-drop events. The OnLButtonUp handler fires when the player releases a dragged item or ability over the button. Without this attribute the button does not receive drop events.

confidence.rationale: draganddrop attribute on Button exercised at runtime. Drop events (OnLButtonUp) fired correctly when attribute was present. User confirmed the drag-and-drop feature works end-to-end.

evidence.summary: APA_FTDropSlotTemplate Button declared with draganddrop="true" received ability drops from the action bar in AdvancedPetAssist. The OnLButtonUp handler fired and the ability ID was correctly captured via Cursor.Data.ObjectId. User confirmed the drag-and-drop cast blacklist feature works.

criteria: Validate whether non-Button element types also support draganddrop if evidence is found in other addons.
promotion.notes: The draganddrop XML attribute is not documented anywhere in docs/war-api or seed files. This is the first documented fact about it.

source.context: APA_FTDropSlotTemplate XML Button with draganddrop=true used in cast blacklist drop slot. User successfully dropped abilities onto the button in-game.

review.notes: Runtime-validated in AdvancedPetAssist. User confirmed UI and behavior work correctly.
